title: First CAR T for solid tumors - Nature Biotechnology
text:
Access through your institution

Buy or subscribe

China’s drug regulator has given a green light to CARsgen Therapeutics’ satri-cel (satricabtagene autoleucel) for gastric cancer, which makes it the very first chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cell therapy approved for solid tumors.CAR T cell therapies have been transformative against hematological malignancies, but so far drug developers have struggled to extend their potential to solid tumors. One reason is that solid tumors usually don’t express an antigen on their cell surface that can be recognized by CAR T cells. Satri-cel overcomes this challenge by targeting claudin 18.2 (CLDN18.2), a tight junction protein that is exposed on the surface of gastric epithelial cells when they become cancerous and the tight junctions between cells break down. In healthy cells, CLDN18.2 helps to pull neighboring cells together, and so CLDN18.2 is hidden in the cell–cell junction.
